{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What scope of improvement do you want? A) Copyediting and style polish only (sentence/paragraph level). B) Polish plus conceptual reframing and section-level reorganization, including checking logical consistency and evidence-claim alignment. C) B plus novelty positioning versus prior work (e.g., S4, Mamba, iTransformer) and adding/adjusting citations.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The draft’s core claim (ATA as attention vs. a state-space formulation) may need conceptual reframing to present a coherent thesis and contribution.",
    "R1.2": "Strengthening the argument chain from the quadratic-attention limitation to the proposed linear mechanism is key to internal logical consistency.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about performance and generalization should be aligned precisely with the reported ETTh1 MSE numbers and ablation outcomes.",
    "R1.5": "Section- and subsection-level ordering may need adjustment to present motivation, method, and results in a clear narrative arc.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and tone are central to making the draft more readable and professional.",
    "RX.1": "Improvements should transparently note uncertainties and limits (e.g., single benchmark, horizon-specific results).",
    "RX.2": "Edits must avoid overstating results or generalization beyond what the reported evidence supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain within drafting and exposition improvements without introducing new experiments or unverifiable claims.",
    "RX.4": "Wording should avoid misleading readers or encouraging misuse of results, particularly around claimed advantages.",
    "RX.5": "Any strengthened claims or attributions should be traceable to the provided text and clearly attributable to cited prior work."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What scope of improvement do you want? A) Copyediting and style polish only (sentence/paragraph level). B) Polish plus conceptual reframing and section-level reorganization, including checking logical consistency and evidence-claim alignment. C) B plus novelty positioning versus prior work (e.g., S4, Mamba, iTransformer) and adding/adjusting citations.

rationale.R1.1: The draft’s core claim (ATA as attention vs. a state-space formulation) may need conceptual reframing to present a coherent thesis and contribution.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening the argument chain from the quadratic-attention limitation to the proposed linear mechanism is key to internal logical consistency.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about performance and generalization should be aligned precisely with the reported ETTh1 MSE numbers and ablation outcomes.
rationale.R1.5: Section- and subsection-level ordering may need adjustment to present motivation, method, and results in a clear narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and tone are central to making the draft more readable and professional.
rationale.RX.1: Improvements should transparently note uncertainties and limits (e.g., single benchmark, horizon-specific results).
rationale.RX.2: Edits must avoid overstating results or generalization beyond what the reported evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain within drafting and exposition improvements without introducing new experiments or unverifiable claims.
rationale.RX.4: Wording should avoid misleading readers or encouraging misuse of results, particularly around claimed advantages.
rationale.RX.5: Any strengthened claims or attributions should be traceable to the provided text and clearly attributable to cited prior work.
